article_title: Fanuc Bets on Physical AI and US Manufacturing With Nvidia Partnership and $90 Million Michigan Expansion

content_preview.title: Fanuc Bets on Physical AI and US Manufacturing With Nvidia Partnership and $90 Million Michigan Expansion
content_preview.summary: Fanuc is integrating Nvidia's simulation and inference platforms across its industrial robot portfolio while committing $90 million to a new 840,000-square-foot Michigan facility, positioning the world's largest robot maker to meet surging North American demand for AI-driven factory automation.
content_preview.body_excerpt: Fanuc, the world's largest manufacturer of industrial robots, is making a two-pronged push to embed artificial intelligence across its product line and expand its domestic production capacity. The Japanese automation giant announced a collaboration with Nvidia to integrate physical AI technologies into robots spanning payloads from 3 kilograms to 2.3 tons, while separately committing [90 million dollars to build a new 840,000-square-foot manufacturing facility](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-re...

editor_notes.content_quality: Good industry news combining two related Fanuc announcements with GTC 2026 context.
editor_notes.source_verification: PRNewswire Fanuc+Nvidia (fetched): Confirms Isaac Sim, Omniverse, Jetson, 3kg-2.3 ton range, digital twin, AI inference. PRNewswire Fanuc America (fetched): Confirms $90M, Michigan, 840,000 sq ft, late 2027, 225 jobs. NVIDIA Newsroom (fetched): Confirms ABB/Kuka/Universal Robots/Yaskawa, 2M+ installed robots, GTC 2026 physical AI push.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified across all 3 sources.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Fanuc coverage. Unique topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid robotics industry news. Ready for publication.
